issues.title: CopilotPanel chat messages lack ARIA live region and keyboard navigation
issues.description: The chat message list has no aria-live region so screen readers never announce new messages. Messages are not keyboard navigable. The input textarea lacks an accessible label. This violates PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards for accessibility (axe-core zero critical violations, keyboard navigable, aria-live for status changes).
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: Find the scrollable messages container div. BEFORE: It has no ARIA attributes and messages are just rendered divs with no roles. The textarea input has a placeholder but no associated label element or aria-label. AFTER: Add aria-live='polite' and role='log' to the messages scroll container. Add aria-label='AI Copilot conversation' to the same container. Add role='article' and aria-label with the message sender to each AgentMessage wrapper. Add aria-label='Message to AI Copilot' to the AgentMentionInput's textarea (pass it through as a prop). Add a visually hidden h2 'AI Copilot' at the top of the panel for screen reader landmark navigation. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Dashboard AI insights panel shows only fallback starter data, never real project metrics
issues.description: The getAiInsights function falls back to hardcoded 'starterInsights' when both AI service and Supabase return empty results. There is no logic to compute real derived insights from existing project data (RFI counts, budget variance, schedule status). The Dashboard page criterion #1 requires real calculated metrics, not placeholders. The AI Copilot acceptance criteria require answers sourced from actual project data.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/ai.ts, in the getAiInsights function: Find the block that creates starterInsights when cachedInsights.length === 0 && budgetInsights.length === 0. BEFORE: It returns 3 hardcoded onboarding insights with static text regardless of project state. AFTER: Before creating starter insights, query real project data: (1) count open RFIs from supabase.from('rfis').select('id', {count:'exact'}).eq('project_id', projectId).eq('status','open'), (2) count overdue RFIs where due_date < now and status != closed, (3) count open punch list items. If any counts are > 0, generate computed insights like 'You have X open RFIs, Y are overdue' with type 'rfi_status', severity based on overdue count (critical if >5, warning if >0, info if 0). Only fall back to onboarding starter insights if ALL counts are zero (truly empty project). Wrap each query in try/catch so failures don't break the function. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: FloatingAIButton and CopilotPanel are not optimized for mobile field use
issues.description: The floating AI button uses spacing['6'] (24px) from bottom/right which can overlap mobile bottom navigation. The button is 48px (spacing['12']) which meets touch targets but the panel it opens (CopilotPanel) does not adapt to mobile width. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires bottom sheet modals on mobile and 44x44px minimum touch targets. The panel opens as a side panel on all viewports instead of a bottom sheet on mobile.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component: BEFORE: The button has fixed position with bottom: spacing['6'] and right: spacing['6'] on all screen sizes. No media query or responsive adjustment. AFTER: Add a check for window.innerWidth < 768 (use a state variable initialized from window.innerWidth and updated on resize). When on mobile, change bottom to spacing['20'] (80px) to clear the mobile bottom navigation bar. Change right to spacing['4'] (16px) for tighter mobile margins. Keep the button size at spacing['12'] (48px) which already meets the 44px minimum touch target. Add aria-label that includes the insight count: `${insightCount} AI insights. Open AI Copilot`. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AgentMentionInput silently swallows send failures with no user feedback
issues.description: The onSend callback is called but if the parent's send function throws (network failure, rate limit, AI service down), the AgentMentionInput has no error state and no way to show the user what happened. The input clears on send regardless of success. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires toast notifications on API failures and inline retry buttons on network errors.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx, in the AgentMentionInput component: Find the handleSend function (or equivalent submit handler). BEFORE: It calls onSend(value.trim()), immediately clears the input with setValue(''), and has no error handling. If onSend throws, the message is lost. AFTER: Wrap the onSend call in a try/catch. Before calling onSend, save the current value to a local variable. On success, clear the input. On error, do NOT clear the input so the user can retry. Import toast from 'sonner' and call toast.error('Failed to send message. Please try again.') in the catch block. Also add a disabled state while sending: create a local isSending state, set it true before onSend, false after (in finally). Disable the send button and textarea while isSending is true. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: CopilotPanel does not subscribe to real-time AI conversation updates from other users
issues.description: The CopilotPanel renders conversation messages from local state only. There is no Supabase real-time subscription on ai_messages or ai_conversations tables. If another user (or a background AI agent) adds a message to the same conversation, the current user never sees it. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require any entity modified by another user to reflect within 2 seconds via Supabase Realtime subscription. The FloatingAIButton subscribes to ai_insights changes but the actual conversation panel does not.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: Find the useEffect hooks near the top of the component. BEFORE: There is no Supabase real-time subscription for ai_messages table changes. Messages only update from local state after the user sends a message and receives a response. AFTER: Add a useEffect that creates a Supabase real-time channel subscription. Import supabase from '../../lib/supabase'. Subscribe to postgres_changes on the 'ai_messages' table filtered by the current conversation_id (if one exists). On INSERT events, check if the new message is not from the current user (compare message.user_id to the authenticated user id), and if so, append the new message to the local messages state. Use supabase.channel(`copilot-messages-${conversationId}`).on('postgres_changes', { event: 'INSERT', schema: 'public', table: 'ai_messages', filter: `conversation_id=eq.${conversationId}` }, callback).subscribe(). Return a cleanup function that calls supabase.removeChannel. Only subscribe when conversationId is truthy. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

invented_features.title: Proactive Morning Briefing
invented_features.description: At 6 AM project time, the AI Copilot auto-generates a 'Project Pulse' — a one paragraph natural language summary of project health combining yesterday's daily log, weather forecast impact on today's schedule, overdue RFIs, and budget burn rate. Delivered as the first message when the superintendent opens the dashboard. No competitor does this. Procore Assist answers questions; SiteSync tells you what to worry about before you ask.

competitive_intel: Procore Assist is a reactive chatbot that searches existing docs. SiteSync's multi-agent architecture (schedule, cost, safety, quality, compliance, document specialists) with generative UI components (charts, approval cards, data tables rendered inline) is architecturally ahead. However, Procore's agent builder lets users create custom AI workflows without code. SiteSync needs the real-time data pipeline working (insights derived from live project data, not stubs) to deliver on the 'AI is the foundation' promise. Autodesk Construction IQ does predictive risk but only for BIM-heavy projects. SiteSync's field-first approach with voice input and mobile copilot is a unique wedge.
summary: The Project Intelligence module has strong architectural bones — multi-agent orchestrator, generative UI components (14+ block types), specialist agent routing, and a well-designed CopilotPanel. The differentiation score is high because no competitor ships inline approval cards, cost breakdowns, and schedule cards inside a chat interface. However, the module is undermined by three critical gaps: (1) insights are hardcoded fallbacks instead of computed from real project data, making the AI feel like a demo, (2) accessibility is poor with no ARIA live regions or keyboard navigation in the chat, violating acceptance criteria, and (3) real-time subscriptions are missing from the conversation panel so multi-user AI collaboration doesn't work. The mobile experience needs repositioning to avoid overlapping bottom navigation. Error handling in the input component silently loses messages on failure. Fixing these five issues would move the score from 58 to the 72-78 range and make the AI copilot feel like it actually knows the project.
